narrative: You notice a dense low growing mat of bright green leaves sprawling across the woodland floor...
interesting_fact: Seeds were historically fed to caged birds — that is where the common name Chickweed comes from.

questions.question: Does Chickweed have any other common names?
What is the botanical name of Chickweed?
Where is Chickweed typically found?
options: Almost anywhere — gardens, woodland edges, disturbed ground and wasteland
Rocky coastal paths and cliff tops
Dry chalky grassland in full sun
questions.correct: Almost anywhere — gardens, woodland edges, disturbed ground and wasteland

questions.question: Select all key identifying features of Chickweed
options: Small oval leaves growing in opposite pairs on slender stems
A single line of hairs running up one side of the stem alternating sides at each node
Tiny hooked hairs that cling to clothing
Kidney shaped leaves with rounded toothed edges
correct: Small oval leaves growing in opposite pairs on slender stems
A single line of hairs running up one side of the stem alternating sides at each node

questions.question: What is Chickweed used for? Select all that apply
What are the risks or cautions with Chickweed?
options: Contains saponins — fine in normal quantities but excess can cause digestive upset
Toxic to humans even in small amounts
Can cause severe skin irritation on contact
No risks whatsoever — eat as much as you like
questions.correct: Contains saponins — fine in normal quantities but excess can cause digestive upset

questions.question: Which species could Chickweed be confused with?
When does Chickweed flower?
options: Year round in mild weather — tiny white star shaped flowers
questions.correct: Year round in mild weather — tiny white star shaped flowers

questions.question: What time of year is Chickweed most commonly found?
options: Year round — best in spring when leaves are young and tender
questions.correct: Year round — best in spring when leaves are young and tender

questions.question: Is Chickweed native to the UK?
options: It was introduced by Roman settlers

questions.question: What was Chickweed historically used to treat? Select all that apply
How was Chickweed applied medicinally? Select all that apply
options: As a poultice applied to itchy or inflamed skin
As a tea or infusion for chest complaints
Ground into a powder and swallowed dry
correct: As a poultice applied to itchy or inflamed skin
As a tea or infusion for chest complaints

questions.question: What is the life cycle of Chickweed?
options: Biennial — takes two years to complete
Ephemeral — only lasts a few weeks

questions.question: What soil or habitat conditions does Chickweed prefer?
options: Moist fertile soil — grows almost anywhere
Dry sandy soil in full sun
questions.correct: Moist fertile soil — grows almost anywhere

questions.question: What plant family does Chickweed belong to?
options: Caryophyllaceae — the pink or carnation family
questions.correct: Caryophyllaceae — the pink or carnation family

questions.question: What is the origin of the name Stellaria?
options: From Latin stella meaning star — referring to the star shaped flowers
Named after the botanist Heinrich Stellar
From Greek meaning small and creeping
questions.correct: From Latin stella meaning star — referring to the star shaped flowers

questions.question: What does Media mean in the botanical name Stellaria media?
options: Middle or intermediate in Latin — referring to its medium size
Medicinal in Latin — referring to its herbal uses
Meadow in Latin — referring to its habitat
Small in Greek — referring to its tiny flowers
questions.correct: Middle or intermediate in Latin — referring to its medium size
